content.full_text:
April 30, 1993


Charles H. Bell, Jr.
Bell & Hiltachk
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 530
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-93-121

Dear Mr. Bell:

You have requested advice on behalf of Sandra R. Smoley,
Secretary of the State Consumer and Servicing Agency, regarding the
conflict-of-interest provisions and her duties under the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Your letter does not seek advice regarding a specific pending
governmental decision and your question is general in nature.
Accordingly, we treat your letter as a request for informal
assistance pursuant to the provisions of Regulation 18239.²


QUESTION

Ms. Smoley has a financial interest in Carissimi-Rohrer &
Associates and its clients, and Richards Boulevard Partners and
Grove Investments and the individuals who comprise these two
entities. If Ms. Smoley disqualifies herself from participating
in any agency decision-making that may foreseeably affect any of
the above, is she in compliance with the requirements of the
Political Reform Act?


_______________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-93-121
Page 2


CONCLUSION

     If Ms. Smoley disqualifies herself from participating in
making or using her official position to influence any
governmental decision which will have a foreseeable material
financial effect on Carissimi-Rohrer & Associates and any of its
clients, and Richards Boulevard Partners and Grove Investments and
the individuals who comprise these two entities, she will be in
compliance with the Act.

FACTS

     Ms. Smoley serves as the Secretary of the State Consumer and
Services Agency ("agency"). Her husband, Walter Rohrer, is a
principal and 25 percent owner in the architectural firm of
Carissimi-Rohrer & Associates, located in Sacramento, California.
For the last five years, Carissimi-Rohrer & Associates has
performed architectural and space design services for Richards
Boulevard Associates and Grove Investments, which develop office
buildings in the Sacramento area. Richards Boulevard Partners,
Grove Investments, or both, entered into long-term space lease
arrangements as lessor to the State of California prior to Ms.
Smoley's appointment as Secretary of the Consumer and Services
Agency in late 1992.

     It is Ms. Smoley's understanding that Grove Investments or
Richards Boulevard Partners intends to soon enter into
negotiations with the State Department of General Services to
lease office buildings, on a long term, build-to-suit basis from
the State of California. Carissimi-Rohrer & Associates will be
the architectural and design services vendor for this project.
The Real Estate Division of the State Department of General
Services is responsible to negotiate and secure office space for
almost all agencies of state government. The Consumer and
Services Agency provides general supervision of the State
Department of General Services ("department"). Although the
director of the department is the official responsible for
decision-making for such space lease negotiations and for state
contracting policies, upon occasion a policy issue of this nature
may be subject to agency-level decision-making.

     Although Ms. Smoley has no information about the likelihood
that negotiations will actually lead to the execution of such a
lease agreement, she believes that she has a financial interest
which would be materially affected by such decisions. In an
abundance of caution, Ms. Smoley has directed that her senior
staff not participate in the making of such decisions due to the
appearance of a conflict of interest.

     Ms. Smoley intends to disqualify herself from participating
in any decision-making on policy matters which may affect general
contracting policies of the Department of General Services, the
Consumer and Services Agency or contract negotiations relative to
matters which foreseeably affect her financial interests.

Our File No. I-93-121
Page 3


     Ms. Smoley intends to disqualify herself from participating
in any decision-making on policy matters which may affect general
contracting policies of the Department of General Services, the
Consumer and Services Agency or contract negotiations relative to
matters which foreseeably affect her financial interests.
Specifically, she intends to disqualify herself from participating
in any decision-making or contract negotiations in matters in
which her husband, Walter Rohrer and Carissimi-Rohrer & Associates
or its clients may have policy issues before, or contracts under
negotiation with, the agency or the department.

                              ANALYSIS

     Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in or using their official position to influence a
governmental decision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest.

     An official has a financial interest in a governmental deci-
sion within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial ef-
fect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on
the official, or on a member of the official's immediate family,
or on any of the financial interests listed in Section 87103.

     Pursuant to Section 82030(a), "income of an individual also
includes a pro rata share of any income of any business entity or
trust in which the individual or spouse owns, directly, indirectly
or beneficially, a 10-percent interest or greater." Ms. Smoley's
husband is a principal and 25-percent owner of Carissimi-Rohrer &
Associates. Therefore, any of the clients of Carissimi-Rohrer &
Associates are considered a source of income to Ms. Smoley.

     We we do not have any facts regarding the identity of the
individuals who comprise Richards Boulevard Partners and Grove
Investments. Since both entities are clients of Carissimi-Rohrer
& Associates, the individuals in these entities are also a source
of income to Ms. Smoley.

     Ms. Smoley must disqualify herself from participating in any
decision which will have a foreseeable material financial effect
on Carissimi-Rohrer & Associates and any of its clients, Richards
Boulevard Partners and Grove Investments and the individuals who
comprise these two entities. The term "participating in making a
governmental decision" is broadly defined in Regulation 18700. In
addition, she may not use her official position to "influence"
(defined in Regulation 18700.1) a governmental decision which will
have a material financial effect on any of the above.

     If Ms. Smoley disqualifies herself and neither "participates
in making" nor "influences" a governmental decision concerning the

Our File No. I-93-121
Page 4


issues identified in this letter, she will be in compliance with
the Act. As discussed, she also may not participate in making any
decision which will have a material financial effect on any source
of income to her.

     I trust this answers your question.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Jeff Marschner
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Jill Stecher
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

     Secretary
     Consumer and Services Agency
     915 Capitol Mall, Suite 200
     Sacramento, California  95814
